title: viola × haynaldii
intro: Viola × haynaldii, also known as the hybrid violet or Hungarian violet, is a cross between Viola reichenbachiana and Viola alpina, and is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Viola × haynaldii include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin for its therapeutic benefits.
